title: lonicera caucasica subsp. govaniana
intro: Lonicera caucasica subsp. govaniana, also known as the Caucasian honeysuckle or govaniana honeysuckle, is a plant species in the Honeysuckle family (Caprifoliaceae). It is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Caucasus region, known for its medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and flowers have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and digestive issues, due to their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. In herbal preparations, Lonicera caucasica subsp. govaniana is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate colds, sore throats, and other respiratory ailments.
